mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 138,037 thousand suggests sub-trend employment growth
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with expanding labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 5.0% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% as of t-1mo indicates elevated policy rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.8%, or Rank IC reverses sign over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1.5% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,381 thousand and RSAFS at 377,615 million as of t-2mo
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with synchronized demand weakness
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails to outperform single-series components (Rank IC of interaction < max(IC_RSAFS, IC_HOUST)) over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth above 0.3% month-over-month signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: economic expansion with rising industrial activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals the Federal Reserve will maintain restrictive policy, reducing equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 208.028 and FEDFUNDS at 5.02% as of t-2mo and t-1mo respectively
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with persistent inflation and tight monetary policy
failure_condition: Interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns are positive in > 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of t-1mo, below current trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising joblessness
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative UNRATE crossings fail to produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings potential, which increases equity risk appetite and valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,523.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of t-5mo
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 62% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,200 thousand units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) stagnation signals demand-side weakness and reduced forward economic momentum, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,200 thousand AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,381 thousand suggests above-trigger level as of t-2mo
regime_hint: demand slowdown with synchronized weakness in housing and retail
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression, or 5-day returns are positive in > 58% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.
